history.msg: Critic PASSED. Required sections (3/4/5/10) present and well over word-count floors. No forward-reference violations against 3.3's own concepts_introduced or 3.1/3.2 in sequence.index.json; one non-blocking watch item noted (descriptive, non-syntactic mention of ModuleNotFoundError well before topic 7.2 Errors & Exceptions -- no try/except syntax used or required). Independently re-verified (not just trusted) the corpus-builder's claim that Poetry/Pytest content stays at concept level and venv makes no live-terminal assumption: re-read sections 5.4-5.6/6/9 and grepped the whole corpus for fixture|unittest|TestCase|conftest|parametrize|@pytest|self.assert patterns -- zero hits; claim holds. Length: independent manual recount ~2328 words excluding code blocks (~2390-2400 including them), corroborating the corpus-builder's own ~2400 self-report; ~8.6-9.1% over the deep profile's 2200-word upper bound (artifact-mix.json), a smaller overage than 3.2's ~11.8% and well inside the 30% hard-fail tolerance -- soft flag, non-blocking, same treatment 3.2 received. Full report: critic-reports/corpus.critic.json. Generated bias-defensive GIFT quiz (artifacts/quiz.gift + artifacts/quiz.meta.json): 14 questions (11 MCQ + 3 short-answer), all 6 corpus Learning Objectives covered at least once (import forms/namespacing x2, random/math/datetime x3, pip-vs-stdlib x2, venv workflow x2 MCQ + 1 SA, poetry x1 MCQ + 1 SA, pytest x1 MCQ + 1 SA). Shuffle re-derived independently from the written .gift's =/~ markers (not trusted from self-report): correct-position distribution across the 11 MCQs is [2,0,3,1,0,2,3,1,2,0,3] -> counts {0:3, 1:2, 2:3, 3:3}, the closest-to-uniform integer split possible for 11 items over 4 positions. Option-length balance independently hand-recounted char-by-char from the actual answer-line text (not just meta claims): this recount caught and fixed a genuine arithmetic slip in the first-draft Q1 tally (had been 95/[115,108,104], corrected to 94/[105,99,94]); final verified ratios range 0.935x-1.138x of distractor mean across all 11 MCQs, none exceeding the skill's 1.20x flag threshold. $bias_defense_applied=true set only after this verification pass. [hand-edited: no Bash tool available to this subagent, progress_update.py not run -- applied atomic_write_json's 2-space indent + trailing newline + history-entry pattern manually]

Poetry/Pytest/venv content confirmed concept-level: no fixtures, no unittest.TestCase, no conftest/parametrize, venv workflow explicitly framed as non-live. Faithfulness: 0/12 sampled assertions unsupported -- close paraphrase of approved corpus. Tone plainspoken, 0 hedging phrases (one long outlier sentence noted, non-blocking). 6-node tooling-pipeline diagram placed at top of Worked Example as a capstone synthesizing all six Key Concepts subsections -- judged correct placement (diagram scope is whole-pipeline, matches artifact_plan.diagram_intent verbatim; anchoring to one Key Concepts subsection would understate its scope), not a defect. Quiz critic PASSED. Independently re-derived correct-position sequence from quiz.gift's =/~ markers (not trusted from meta/builder self-report): [2,0,3,1,0,2,3,1,2,0,3] -> counts {0:3,1:2,2:3,3:3} matches sidecar exactly; max_share=3/11=0.2727 < 0.40 threshold, and this split is judged the closest-to-uniform integer distribution achievable for 11 items over 4 slots (11/4=2.75) -- not flagged as bias. Independently hand-counted every answer-line's character length (feedback stripped) from the raw .gift and computed len(correct)/mean(distractors) per the skill's actual rule: all 11 MCQs land at 0.935x-1.138x, none exceeding the 1.20x flag threshold, so longest_correct_rate under the governing rule is 0/11=0.0 (well under the 0.40 fail threshold). Builder's separately-reported 'correct is literally the longest option in 3/11 (~27%)' is a different, non-blocking metric and was confirmed accurate (q2, q4, q6) but does not drive pass/fail under this skill. Distractor plausibility: no absurd (score-1) distractors found, absurd_rate=0.0. Objective coverage: all 6 corpus Learning Objectives (section 3) matched to >=1 question each, no orphan objective_ref values. Full report: critic-reports/quiz.critic.json. drift-critic skill run across module 1-functions-modules-tooling-2 (topics 3.1, 3.2, 3.3): wrote critic-reports/module-drift.critic.json at the module directory. All 5 checks passed: terminology consistent (e.g. 3.3's 'namespacing' is a new, module-scoped term that does not collide with 3.1's variable-scope terminology); prerequisite ordering respected (3.3's own Prerequisites section cites only 3.1, 2.2, and 1.1, all earlier positions; 3.3 never assumes lambdas/decorators/generators from 3.2); no duplicated Core Concepts across the 3 topics; learning arc fully covers narrative_seed_by_week[3] (modules & imports/pip/venv/Poetry/Pytest all delivered here); lab_activity_seed_by_week[3]'s 'stdlib imports' and 'venv + pip + Poetry + Pytest' labs map cleanly to this topic's Hands-On Exercise (dice.py + random.randint) and Implementation/Worked Example walkthroughs, appropriately kept conceptual/offline per the source_outline's own '(concept)' tags. One minor non-blocking finding was raised against 3.2 (Fibonacci-generator lab substitution), none against 3.3. Informational only, non-blocking: week-3 expected_delivery_minutes totals 30/90 across the module, outside weekly_balance_rule's +/-25% band per artifact-mix.json -- a course-drift-critic concern, already flagged individually at HITL#3, repeated here for visibility only. Verdict: passed.
